parameters.query:
-- Check for duplicate upload
SELECT id FROM upload_log WHERE file_hash = '{{ $json.file_hash }}' LIMIT 1;

-- If no duplicate, insert upload log
INSERT INTO upload_log (file_name, file_hash, row_count, status)
SELECT 'webhook_upload', '{{ $json.file_hash }}', {{ $json.raw_punch_count }}, 'processing'
WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM upload_log WHERE file_hash = '{{ $json.file_hash }}');

-- Upsert daily record (idempotent)
INSERT INTO daily_records (employee_id, name, date, check_in, check_out, late_flag, missing_punch, raw_punch_count)
VALUES (
  '{{ $json.employee_id }}',
  '{{ $json.name }}',
  '{{ $json.date }}',
  {{ $json.check_in ? "'" + $json.check_in + "'" : 'NULL' }},
  {{ $json.check_out ? "'" + $json.check_out + "'" : 'NULL' }},
  {{ $json.late_flag }},
  {{ $json.missing_punch }},
  {{ $json.raw_punch_count }}
)
ON CONFLICT (employee_id, date) DO UPDATE SET
  check_in = EXCLUDED.check_in,
  check_out = EXCLUDED.check_out,
  late_flag = EXCLUDED.late_flag,
  missing_punch = EXCLUDED.missing_punch,
  raw_punch_count = EXCLUDED.raw_punch_count;

-- Upsert monthly summary
INSERT INTO monthly_summary (employee_id, name, month_year, late_count, last_late_date)
VALUES (
  '{{ $json.employee_id }}',
  '{{ $json.name }}',
  '{{ $json.month_year }}',
  CASE WHEN {{ $json.late_flag }} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END,
  CASE WHEN {{ $json.late_flag }} THEN '{{ $json.date }}' ELSE NULL END
)
ON CONFLICT (employee_id, month_year) DO UPDATE SET
  late_count = CASE WHEN {{ $json.late_flag }} THEN monthly_summary.late_count + 1 ELSE monthly_summary.late_count END,
  last_late_date = CASE WHEN {{ $json.late_flag }} THEN '{{ $json.date }}' ELSE monthly_summary.last_late_date END;
SELECT late_count, warning_level FROM monthly_summary WHERE employee_id = '{{ $json.employee_id }}' AND month_year = '{{ $json.month_year }}'

values.content:
You are an AI assistant embedded in an enterprise HR automation system. You write attendance warning emails on behalf of the HR department at Growify.

RULES:
1. Output ONLY a JSON object with two keys: "subject" and "body".
2. "subject" must be a concise email subject line (max 100 chars).
3. "body" must be the email body in plain text, ready to send.
4. Address the EMPLOYEE directly.
5. Sign off as "HR Team, Growify".
6. Never fabricate facts.

OUTPUT FORMAT (strict JSON):
{"subject": "...", "body": "..."}

additionalFields.description: Mandatory attendance review meeting for {{ $json.name }} ({{ $json.employee_id }}). This is a 3rd strike escalation.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO warning_log (employee_id, name, month_year, strike_level, email_subject, email_body, delivery_status)
VALUES (
  '{{ $json.employee_id }}',
  '{{ $json.name }}',
  '{{ $json.month_year }}',
  {{ $json.late_count }},
  '{{ JSON.parse($json.ai_response).subject }}',
  '{{ JSON.parse($json.ai_response).body }}',
  'sent'
);

UPDATE monthly_summary SET
  warning_level = {{ $json.late_count }},
  last_warning_date = '{{ $json.date }}'
WHERE employee_id = '{{ $json.employee_id }}' AND month_year = '{{ $json.month_year }}';

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, processed: $input.all().length, message: 'Attendance records processed successfully.' }) }}
